summary: CloudWatch alarms and log groups. L1s adjust alarm thresholds and log retention within bounds — LOW risk, reversible, auto-eligible.

operations.title: Adjust a CloudWatch alarm threshold
operations.summary: Change the value that triggers this alarm. Updates instantly with no downtime.
operations.description: Change the threshold of an existing metric alarm within bounds. In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: Which alarm to adjust.

params.name: new_threshold
params.label: New threshold
params.help: Between 1 and 100.

operations.title: Change log-group retention
operations.summary: Change how many days logs are kept before they're automatically deleted. No downtime.
operations.description: Set the retention period of a log group to an allowlisted number of days.

params.name: log_group
params.label: Log group
params.help: The CloudWatch log group whose retention to change, picked from the estate inventory so only existing Terraform-managed log groups can be selected.

params.name: retention_in_days
params.label: Retention (days)
params.help: How many days CloudWatch keeps log events before automatically deleting them; limited to standard AWS retention values from 7 to 365 days so logs are never kept forever or purged faster than a week.

operations.title: Delete a CloudWatch alarm
operations.summary: Delete this alarm. Monitoring stops right away, but it can be recreated from code later.
operations.description: Remove a metric alarm. Reversible — it can be re-created from code. Low blast radius.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm to remove, picked from the estate inventory; because the alarm definition lives in Terraform code it can be re-created later, keeping the blast radius low.

operations.title: Update dashboard JSON (widgets / layout)
operations.summary: Replace this dashboard's widgets and layout with new JSON; an engineer authors and reviews it first.
operations.description: Replace dashboard_body with a new CloudWatch dashboard JSON document (widgets, layout, metric expressions). Updates in place, reversible, no downtime. The body is free-form JSON with no provider-side schema validation, so it cannot be reduced to a bounded form — an engineer authors/reviews the JSON.

params.name: dashboard
params.label: Dashboard
params.help: The CloudWatch dashboard whose JSON body will be replaced, picked from the estate inventory so only existing Terraform-managed dashboards can be edited.

params.name: dashboard_body
params.label: New dashboard JSON
params.help: Full CloudWatch dashboard JSON. Free-form and unbounded, which is why an engineer authors/reviews it rather than this being a self-service form.

operations.title: Rename dashboard
operations.summary: Rename this dashboard. Applying REPLACES it — a brief gap, and the old dashboard link stops working.
operations.description: Change dashboard_name. Dashboard names cannot be edited in place — Terraform destroys the old dashboard and creates a new one (brief gap; the old dashboard URL stops working). The new name is pattern-bounded (letters, digits, hyphens, underscores; max 255). Reversible by renaming back; content is preserved in Terraform config.

params.name: dashboard
params.label: Dashboard
params.help: The CloudWatch dashboard to rename, picked from the estate inventory; note that renaming destroys and recreates the dashboard, so the old URL stops working.

params.name: new_name
params.label: New dashboard name
params.help: Letters, numbers, hyphens and underscores only; max 255 chars. Renaming recreates the dashboard (brief gap, old URL breaks).

operations.title: Move dashboard to another Region
operations.summary: Move this dashboard to another Region. Applying REPLACES it — a brief gap, and widgets tied to the old Region's metrics may show blank.
operations.description: Change the resource's region (enhanced Region support). Recreates the dashboard in the target Region (brief gap). Bounded to an allowlist of Regions. Caution: widgets that reference metrics in the original Region may render empty after the move. Reversible by moving back.

params.name: dashboard
params.label: Dashboard
params.help: The CloudWatch dashboard to move, picked from the estate inventory so only existing Terraform-managed dashboards can be selected.

params.name: region
params.label: Target Region
params.help: Recreates the dashboard in this Region. Widgets referencing metrics from the old Region may go blank.

operations.title: Delete dashboard
operations.summary: Delete the saved dashboard view, with confirmation. Alarms, metrics, and logs are unaffected.
operations.description: Remove the CloudWatch dashboard resource. No impact to alarms, metrics, logs, or running services — this only deletes the saved view. Reversible by re-applying the Terraform config. Requires explicit confirmation.

params.name: dashboard
params.label: Dashboard
params.help: The CloudWatch dashboard to delete, picked from the estate inventory; only the saved view is removed — the alarms, metrics, and logs it displays are untouched.

params.name: confirm
params.label: Confirm deletion
params.help: Deletes the dashboard view. Reversible by re-applying Terraform; no service impact.

operations.title: Request another change to CloudWatch dashboard
operations.summary: Request any other dashboard change not covered above; an engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any change to a CloudWatch dashboard not offered as a bounded form above. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: dashboard
params.label: Dashboard
params.help: The dashboard to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Toggle log group deletion protection
operations.summary: Turn on or off protection that blocks this log group from being deleted.
operations.description: Enable or disable deletion_protection_enabled on a CloudWatch log group. In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: log_group
params.label: Log group
params.help: The CloudWatch log group whose deletion protection to toggle, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed log groups.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Deletion protection
params.help: true blocks the log group from being deleted until protection is switched off again; a bounded true/false toggle that changes nothing else and is fully reversible.

operations.title: Toggle retaining the log group on destroy
operations.summary: Turn on or off keeping this log group and its stored logs if it's ever destroyed.
operations.description: Enable or disable skip_destroy so Terraform retains the log group and its stored logs when destroyed. In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: log_group
params.label: Log group
params.help: The CloudWatch log group whose skip_destroy setting to change, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed log groups.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Retain on destroy
params.help: true tells Terraform to leave the log group and its stored logs in AWS when the resource is destroyed (only removing it from state); a reversible true/false toggle with no live effect until a destroy actually runs.

operations.title: Set log group KMS encryption key
operations.summary: Encrypt this log group's data with a chosen key. A misconfigured key policy can block log delivery, so it's guardrailed.
operations.description: Associate a KMS key (kms_key_id) with a CloudWatch log group to encrypt log data at rest. In-place and reversible, but a misconfigured key policy can block CloudWatch log delivery — guardrailed.

params.name: log_group
params.label: Log group
params.help: The CloudWatch log group to encrypt at rest, picked from the estate inventory so only existing Terraform-managed log groups can be selected.

params.name: kms_key
params.label: KMS key
params.help: Pick an existing KMS key; its key policy must allow the CloudWatch Logs service to encrypt/decrypt.

operations.title: Add a tag to a log group
operations.summary: Add a tag to this log group. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Add or set a single tag key/value on a CloudWatch log group. Cosmetic metadata, in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: log_group
params.label: Log group
params.help: The CloudWatch log group to tag, picked from the estate inventory; tags are metadata only and never change logging behaviour.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: 1-128 chars: letters, digits, spaces and + - = . _ : / @

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Up to 256 chars: letters, digits, spaces and + - = . _ : / @

operations.title: Remove a tag from a log group
operations.summary: Remove a tag from this log group. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Remove a single tag key from a CloudWatch log group. Cosmetic metadata, in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: log_group
params.label: Log group
params.help: The CloudWatch log group whose tag to remove, picked from the estate inventory; removing a tag is metadata-only and reversible.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Exact tag key currently on the log group.

operations.title: Request another change to CloudWatch Log Group
operations.summary: Request any other log group change, including ones that replace it; an engineer authors the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any CloudWatch log group change not offered as a bounded self-service form — including ForceNew/replacement arguments (name, name_prefix, log_group_class). An engineer authors and applies the Terraform.

params.name: log_group
params.label: Log group
params.help: The log group to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Change alarm comparison operator
operations.summary: Change how the alarm compares its metric to the threshold, for example greater-than or less-than.
operations.description: Set comparison_operator to a standard threshold comparison (how the metric is compared against the threshold). In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose comparison operator to change, picked from the estate inventory so only existing Terraform-managed alarms can be edited.

params.name: comparison_operator
params.label: Comparison operator
params.help: Standard single-threshold comparisons only. Anomaly-detection band operators are excluded because they require threshold_metric_id and extra config.

operations.title: Change alarm statistic
operations.summary: Change which statistic the alarm aggregates, such as average, sum, or maximum.
operations.description: Set the metric statistic (SampleCount/Average/Sum/Minimum/Maximum) the alarm aggregates. In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose aggregation statistic to change, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed alarms.

params.name: statistic
params.label: Statistic
params.help: Applies to single-metric alarms using statistic (not extended_statistic percentiles or metric_query).

operations.title: Toggle alarm actions on/off
operations.summary: Turn alarm notifications on or off. The alarm keeps evaluating either way, it just stays quiet.
operations.description: Enable or disable actions_enabled. When off the alarm still evaluates state but fires no alarm/OK/insufficient-data actions. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose actions to switch on or off, picked from the estate inventory; the alarm itself keeps evaluating either way.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Actions enabled
params.help: false suppresses notifications/actions without deleting the alarm.

operations.title: Send this alarm to a notification topic
operations.summary: Send this alarm's alerts, and optionally its recovery messages, to a notification topic; wiring the wrong topic pages the wrong team.
operations.description: Points the alarm's ALARM-state messages (alarm_actions) at a notification topic — usually the on-call topic — and, when chosen, its recovery messages (ok_actions) too. In-place and reversible; wiring the wrong topic sends pages to the wrong team, so it is guardrailed.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose notifications are being wired, picked from the estate inventory.

params.name: topic
params.label: Notification topic
params.help: Where the alarm's ALARM messages go — usually the on-call topic.

params.name: ok_topic
params.label: Recovery (OK) topic
params.help: Where the all-clear goes when the alarm recovers — usually the same topic. Leave blank to keep the current recovery wiring unchanged.

operations.title: Change how the alarm treats missing data
operations.summary: Change how the alarm treats gaps in data: as missing, healthy, breaching, or ignored.
operations.description: Set treat_missing_data to control how gaps in the metric are evaluated. In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose missing-data handling to change, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed alarms.

params.name: treat_missing_data
params.label: Treat missing data as
params.help: Default is 'missing'. 'breaching' makes gaps trigger the alarm; 'notBreaching' treats gaps as healthy.

operations.title: Change alarm evaluation periods
operations.summary: Change how many time periods the alarm checks before it can trigger.
operations.description: Set evaluation_periods (number of periods over which data is compared to the threshold). In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose evaluation window to change, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed alarms.

params.name: evaluation_periods
params.label: Evaluation periods
params.help: Must be greater than or equal to datapoints_to_alarm. Higher values reduce flapping but slow detection.

operations.title: Change alarm datapoints-to-alarm
operations.summary: Change how many breaching data points within the evaluation window are needed to trigger the alarm.
operations.description: Set datapoints_to_alarm for M-out-of-N alarm evaluation. In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose M-out-of-N trigger setting to change, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed alarms.

params.name: datapoints_to_alarm
params.label: Datapoints to alarm
params.help: Number of breaching datapoints within evaluation_periods required to trigger. Must be less than or equal to evaluation_periods.

operations.title: Change alarm metric period
operations.summary: Change the length of the time window each alarm evaluation covers.
operations.description: Set the metric period in seconds the alarm evaluates over. In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose metric period to change, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed alarms.

params.name: period
params.label: Period (seconds)
params.help: Valid CloudWatch periods: 10/30 (high-resolution) or multiples of 60. Only applies to single-metric alarms, not metric_query alarms.

operations.title: Edit alarm description
operations.summary: Edit the alarm's description text. Cosmetic only, it does not change how the alarm behaves.
operations.description: Update the human-readable alarm_description text. Cosmetic, in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose description text to edit, picked from the estate inventory; the description is cosmetic and does not affect evaluation.

params.name: alarm_description
params.label: Description
params.help: Free text up to 1024 characters describing what the alarm monitors.

operations.title: Add a tag to alarm
operations.summary: Add a tag to this alarm. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Add or set a single key/value entry in the alarm's tags map. In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm to tag, picked from the estate inventory; tags are metadata only and do not change alarm behaviour.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: AWS tag key, up to 128 characters.

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: AWS tag value, up to 256 characters.

operations.title: Remove a tag from alarm
operations.summary: Remove a tag from this alarm. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Remove a single key from the alarm's tags map. In-place update, reversible (re-add with the add-tag form), no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The metric alarm whose tag to remove, picked from the estate inventory; removing a tag is metadata-only and reversible.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Exact key of the tag to remove.

operations.title: Request another change to CloudWatch metric alarm
operations.summary: Request any other change to this alarm not covered above; an engineer authors the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any change to a CloudWatch metric alarm not offered as a bounded self-service form. An engineer reviews and authors the Terraform.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Move alarm to another Region
operations.summary: Move this alarm to another Region. Applying REPLACES it — brief gap, and it may show no data if the metric isn't there too.
operations.description: Change the alarm's region (enhanced Region support). Terraform destroys the alarm and recreates it in the target Region (brief monitoring gap). Bounded to an allowlist of Regions. Caution: the same metric may not exist in the new Region, which can leave the alarm stuck in INSUFFICIENT_DATA. Reversible by moving back.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: Which alarm to move.

params.name: region
params.label: Target Region
params.help: Recreates the alarm in this Region. The alarm's metric must also exist there or the alarm will read INSUFFICIENT_DATA.

operations.title: Change PromQL alarm evaluation interval
operations.summary: Change how often a PromQL alarm re-checks its query, in seconds.
operations.description: Set evaluation_interval, the frequency in seconds at which a PromQL (evaluation_criteria) alarm is evaluated. In-place update, reversible, no downtime. Allowlisted to valid CloudWatch intervals.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: evaluation_interval
params.label: Evaluation interval (seconds)
params.help: Valid values are 10, 20, 30, or any multiple of 60. Applies only to PromQL (evaluation_criteria) alarms.

operations.title: Change alarm extended statistic (percentile)
operations.summary: Change the alarm to evaluate a percentile, like p95 or p99.9, instead of a plain statistic.
operations.description: Set extended_statistic to a percentile between p0.0 and p100 (for example p95 or p99.9). Cannot be combined with statistic. In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: extended_statistic
params.label: Extended statistic (percentile)
params.help: A percentile from p0.0 to p100, e.g. p50, p95, p99.9. Mutually exclusive with statistic.

operations.title: Set alarm anomaly-detection band metric ID
operations.summary: Point the alarm's anomaly-detection band at a specific calculated metric.
operations.description: Set threshold_metric_id to the id of the ANOMALY_DETECTION_BAND metric_query that supplies this alarm's dynamic threshold. Bounded to the CloudWatch metric-query id format. In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: threshold_metric_id
params.label: Anomaly-detection band metric id
params.help: Must match the id of the ANOMALY_DETECTION_BAND metric_query: letters, numbers and underscore, first character a lowercase letter.

operations.title: Change low-sample-count percentile handling
operations.summary: Choose whether a percentile alarm evaluates or ignores low-traffic periods; ignoring can mask real breaches.
operations.description: Set evaluate_low_sample_count_percentiles to control whether a percentile alarm evaluates or ignores periods that have too few samples. 'ignore' holds alarm state on low-confidence data; 'evaluate' always evaluates. Guardrailed because 'ignore' can mask real breaches during low-traffic periods.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: evaluate_low_sample_count_percentiles
params.label: Low-sample percentile handling
params.help: 'ignore' does not change alarm state on periods with few samples; 'evaluate' always evaluates. Used only for percentile-based alarms.

operations.title: Change PromQL alarm pending period
operations.summary: Change how long a PromQL alarm must keep breaching before it goes into alarm state.
operations.description: Set evaluation_criteria.promql_criteria.pending_period, the number of seconds a contributor must be continuously breaching before a PromQL alarm transitions into ALARM. In-place update, reversible, no downtime. Range 0-86400 seconds.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: pending_period
params.label: Pending period (seconds)
params.help: Seconds of continuous breaching before transitioning to ALARM. Valid range 0 to 86400.

operations.title: Change PromQL alarm recovery period
operations.summary: Change how long a PromQL alarm must stop breaching before it recovers to OK.
operations.description: Set evaluation_criteria.promql_criteria.recovery_period, the number of seconds a contributor must be continuously not breaching before a PromQL alarm transitions back to OK. In-place update, reversible, no downtime. Range 0-86400 seconds.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: recovery_period
params.label: Recovery period (seconds)
params.help: Seconds of continuous non-breaching before transitioning back to OK. Valid range 0 to 86400.

operations.title: Change a metric-query period
operations.summary: Change how often one metric calculation in this alarm is sampled, in seconds.
operations.description: Set the period (seconds) of one metric_query block, selected by its id, on a metric-math / Metrics Insights alarm. Allowlisted to valid CloudWatch periods. In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: query_id
params.label: Metric-query id
params.help: The id of the metric_query block to modify: letters, numbers and underscore, first character a lowercase letter.

params.name: period
params.label: Period (seconds)
params.help: Valid CloudWatch periods: 10 or 30 (high-resolution) or multiples of 60.

operations.title: Change a metric-query metric period
operations.summary: Change how often the underlying metric inside one calculation is sampled, in seconds.
operations.description: Set the period (seconds) of the metric inside one metric_query block, selected by the query id, on a metric-math / Metrics Insights alarm. Allowlisted to valid CloudWatch periods. In-place update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: query_id
params.label: Metric-query id
params.help: The id of the metric_query block whose nested metric period you are changing.

params.name: period
params.label: Metric period (seconds)
params.help: Valid CloudWatch periods: 10 or 30 (high-resolution) or multiples of 60.

operations.title: Set a metric-query cross-account source account
operations.summary: Point one metric calculation at another AWS account's data; the wrong account silently breaks the metric.
operations.description: Set account_id on one metric_query block (selected by its id) so that query sources its metrics from another AWS account. 12-digit account ID. In-place update, reversible, no downtime. Guardrailed because pointing at the wrong account silently breaks or misdirects the metric source.

params.name: alarm
params.label: Alarm
params.help: The alarm to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: query_id
params.label: Metric-query id
params.help: The id of the metric_query block to modify: letters, numbers and underscore, first character a lowercase letter.

params.name: account_id
params.label: Source AWS account ID
params.help: 12-digit AWS account ID that holds the metrics for this query.

operations.title: Change log group storage class
operations.summary: Change this log group's storage class. Applying REPLACES it and permanently deletes stored logs unless you keep them on destroy.
operations.description: Set log_group_class to one of the three provider-documented values (STANDARD, INFREQUENT_ACCESS, DELIVERY). This argument is ForceNew in the provider schema (internal/service/logs/group.go): Terraform destroys and recreates the log group on any class change, so by default every stored log event in the existing group is deleted (irreversible unless skip_destroy is set on the resource). Selecting DELIVERY additionally forces retention_in_days to 2. Engineer-authored/reviewed rather than a self-service form: the value set is a bounded enum, but the change is a destructive replacement.

params.name: log_group
params.label: Log group
params.help: Which log group's storage class to change.

params.name: log_group_class
params.label: Log group class
params.help: STANDARD (default full-feature), INFREQUENT_ACCESS (lower-cost, reduced features), or DELIVERY. Changing the class is a ForceNew replacement -- the log group is destroyed and recreated, deleting stored log events unless skip_destroy is set on the resource. DELIVERY additionally forces retention_in_days to 2.

operations.title: Provision a log group
operations.summary: Create a CloudWatch log group with a bounded retention. Logs are deleted permanently when retention lapses.
operations.description: Create an aws_cloudwatch_log_group with a bounded retention period and optional customer-managed encryption. Retention governs how long audit and application logs are kept, so it is chosen from an allowlist. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to review and finish.

params.name: log_group_name
params.label: Log group name
params.help: The name of the log group (written to the name attribute), for example /app/service.

params.name: retention_in_days
params.label: Retention (days)
params.help: How long log events are kept before they are deleted permanently. Choose the period that meets the audit and compliance requirement.

params.name: log_group_class
params.label: Log class
params.help: STANDARD supports all features; INFREQUENT_ACCESS is a lower-cost class for rarely-queried logs.

params.name: kms_key_id
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: An optional customer-managed KMS key to encrypt the log group with (the aws_kms_key.arn it references).

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Description
params.help: A short description recorded as the resource's Description tag.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: The team or person accountable for this resource, recorded as its PIC tag.

decisions: Confirm the retention period meets the audit and compliance requirement: log events are deleted permanently once retention lapses.

operations.title: Provision a metric alarm
operations.summary: Create a CloudWatch metric alarm. An alarm with no action, or the wrong topic, fires silently.
operations.description: Create an aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm watching one metric against a threshold, notifying an SNS topic chosen from inventory. The comparison, statistic, and period are bounded; the metric identity and dimensions are confirmed against the watched resource at review. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to review and finish.

params.name: alarm_name
params.label: Alarm name
params.help: The name of the alarm (written to the alarm_name attribute).

params.name: metric_name
params.label: Metric name
params.help: The metric to watch, for example CPUUtilization. Confirmed against the watched resource at review.

params.name: namespace
params.label: Namespace
params.help: The metric namespace, for example AWS/EC2.

params.name: comparison_operator
params.label: Comparison
params.help: How the statistic is compared to the threshold to decide the alarm state.

params.name: statistic
params.label: Statistic
params.help: The statistic applied to the metric over each period.

params.name: period
params.label: Period (seconds)
params.help: The length of each evaluation period, in seconds.

params.name: evaluation_periods
params.label: Evaluation periods
params.help: How many consecutive periods must breach the threshold before the alarm fires.

params.name: threshold
params.label: Threshold
params.help: The value the statistic is compared against.

params.name: alarm_actions
params.label: Notify topic
params.help: The SNS topic notified when the alarm fires (the aws_sns_topic.arn it references).

params.name: treat_missing_data
params.label: Missing data
params.help: How missing datapoints are treated when deciding the alarm state.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Description
params.help: A short description recorded as the resource's Description tag.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: The team or person accountable for this resource, recorded as its PIC tag.

decisions: Confirm the alarm notifies the right on-call target: an alarm with no action, or the wrong SNS topic, fires silently and no one is paged.
The metric name, namespace, and dimensions must match the resource being watched; the engineer confirms them against the live resource, since a mismatched alarm watches nothing.
